title: Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle
description: Explore the fundamental limits on measurement precision in quantum mechanics. Understand why certain pairs of observables cannot be simultaneously known with arbitrary precision.
learningObjectives: Explain why uncertainty is fundamental, not due to measurement limitations
Identify conjugate variables and their uncertainty relations
Calculate uncertainty bounds for position-momentum and energy-time
Connect uncertainty to non-commuting observables in quantum mechanics
Understand implications for quantum computing and measurement
